assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 13 shows '| Ecosystem | RPM |'. Lines 34-36 state: 'The vulnerable library openssl-libs is an RPM system package. The 2.2.x stream's Ecosystem Mappings table confirms RPM is a configured ecosystem with lock file rpms.lock.yaml and check command...' Ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM based on library name and Ecosystem Mappings config, not Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 8-12 show a table with all five versions: 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4), and 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4). Lines 44-46 confirm data sourced from rpms.lock.yaml: 'The package openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml at each pinned tag in the 2.2.x stream'.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 35-38 show the dependency chain: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install'. Lines 44-46 state: 'The package openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml at each pinned tag in the 2.2.x stream, confirming it is an explicitly installed package (not inherited from the base image).' Lock file presence is explicitly the primary classification signal.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 9 states '## Remediation Task (RPM -- single task)'. Only one jira.create_issue call exists (lines 13-19). The task targets 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (Konflux release repo, line 27). data-extraction.md line 39 confirms: 'RPM ecosystem produces 1 remediation task (Konflux release repo fix only). No upstream backport task is needed.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 25, value: rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (line 29, value: main), '## Description' (line 33), '## Implementation Notes' (line 54), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 64). No '## Files to Modify' section exists anywhere in the task description, confirming intentional omission.

assertion_results.text: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 37 states: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available and external tools prohibited'. Lines 48-51 elaborate: 'SBOM verification status: SBOM comparison was not performed because cosign is not available in this environment and external tool invocation is prohibited. The rpms.lock.yaml classification (explicit install) is used as the sole source of truth for package origin.'
